column1: # max-metica-api-map.tsv — machine-readable source of truth for the MaxSdk ↔
# MeticaSdk API surface. Read by unity-validator (max_api_* rules, in prose)
# and unity-integrator (codegen rewrite + drop pass) so the two stay in lockstep.
# The narrative form of this map lives in references/max-vs-metica-2.4.0-api.md;
# keep them in sync when the SDK bumps.
#   1. pattern      — literal grep -F substring to find in user C# source
#                     (typically `MaxSdk.Method(` with the open paren, or the
#   2. replacement  — the MeticaSdk equivalent the user should write instead,
#                       rename            — direct name swap; signature compatible
#                       signature-change  — Metica equivalent exists but the
#                                           still flags but emits a TODO hint
#                       drop              — no MeticaSdk equivalent; remove the
#                                           call or isolate it behind a Max-only
#   4. notes        — one-line human note; surfaces in validator evidence and
# - `MaxSdkUtils.*` is exempt globally — see the trailing exempt row.
# - Patterns should be as specific as the call site allows; for methods, include
#   the open paren so `MaxSdk.Load` doesn't accidentally match `MaxSdk.LoadBanner(`.
# - Lines starting with `#` and blank lines are skipped by the loader.

column4: Metica owns the AppLovinSdk init; the integrator's own InitializeSdk call must be removed.
UserId moves into MeticaInitConfig constructor — not a runtime setter.
Replace static event subscription with the callback parameter on MeticaSdk.Initialize.
MeticaSdk version drops the Action<MaxCmpError> callback parameter.
Metica banners do NOT auto-refresh by default — call this explicitly after Show.
UnityEngine.Color → hex color string. Convert at the call site (ColorUtility.ToHtmlStringRGB).
APS bid signals (amazon_ad_response / amazon_ad_error) land here.
No MeticaSdk equivalent. Must destroy and recreate the banner to reposition.
No MeticaSdk equivalent for querying banner Rect.
MeticaSdk also offers LoadInterstitial(adUnitId, MeticaAdConfig?) with bid-floor override.
Re-apply after each ad creation — pre-creation calls land on a not-yet-created instance and are silently dropped.
APS bid signals land here. Re-apply after each load.

column1: # App Open Ads — NOT SUPPORTED by MeticaSdk

column4: App Open Ads not supported in MeticaSdk 2.4.0. Remove or keep in a Max-only path.
Global MAX param. Per-format goes through MeticaSdk.Ads.SetXxxExtraParameter.
No MeticaSdk read-back for log state.
No unified MeticaSdk config object; individual properties on MeticaSdk.Ads.Max.
No MeticaSdk equivalent for arbitrary ad-value lookup.
MeticaSdk has its own event system (MeticaSdk.Events.*). Migrate or drop.
MaxSegmentCollection not supported in MeticaSdk; remove targeting via MAX segments.
Action<string,ErrorInfo> → Action<MeticaAdError> (adUnitId now lives on the error).
DisplayFailed → ShowFailed; param order reversed (ad first, error second).
Reload the next ad from this handler — Metica's continuous loader subscribes here too.
Simplified: Action<string,Reward,AdInfo> → Action<MeticaAd>; no Reward.Label / Reward.Amount.
No MeticaSdk equivalent. Alternative: use OnApplicationFocus to pause/resume gameplay.
App Open Ads not supported at all.
No MeticaSdk equivalent (ad review creative ID surfacing).
App Open Ads not supported at all.

column1: # MaxSdkUtils.* are stateless helper functions that don't depend on MaxSdk being
# initialised. They mix-safe inside a Metica integration. The loader filters
# exempt rows by `kind=exempt` before matching, so this row's position in the
# file doesn't affect correctness — it's grouped here for readability.
